market_context.enterprise_fine_tuning_adoption: 72% of organizations plan to fine-tune or use RAGs
market_context.key_stat: By 2027, organizations predicted to use small, task-specific AI 3x more than general LLMs

validated_gaps.title: End-to-End Domain Data Pipeline (Raw Docs to Training Data)

demand_signals.signal: easy-dataset repo (ConardLi) trending: converts domain docs to fine-tuning datasets with parsing, segmentation, cleaning. Validates strong demand for this capability.
Meta released synthetic-data-kit for generating QA pairs from documents. Validates Big Tech recognizes this gap.
DigitalDivideData reports: 'The training data was not as good as the team believed' is #1 cause of enterprise fine-tuning failure.
Augmentoolkit, Distilabel, Data Prep Kit all emerging as independent tools to fill this gap in the pipeline.
Fine-tuning requires high-quality data but 'obtaining and curating this data can be slow and expensive, especially for complex tasks requiring domain expertise'
LLM-Synthetic-Data repo maintains live reading list of 100+ papers on synthetic data generation, updated through 2025.
Distilabel, Curator, and Augmentoolkit all have growing stars for synthetic data generation frameworks.
Blog post: 'Fine-tune a SmolLM on domain-specific synthetic data from a LLM' demonstrates demand for teacher->student synthetic data pipeline.
'A lack of domain-specific datasets is a common problem for smaller and more specialized models' - widely cited challenge.

Developer wanted 'a good interactive demo with a fine-tuned model performing better than the base model, combined with extremely detailed information on how it was fine-tuned'. Evaluation transparency is a pain point.
Developer 'ran up against a timebox allocated for evaluation work' - evaluation is time-consuming and underresourced.
Enterprise fine-tuning failures linked to 'absence of evaluation frameworks that measure what actually matters' in production.
NVIDIA published dedicated blog on 'Fine-Tuning LLMOps for Rapid Model Evaluation' acknowledging evaluation as major gap.
Paper on fine-tuning LLM judges: domain-specific evaluation models needed for 'more granular, accurate and critical feedback'.
'The most valuable source of fine-tuning data is the production data generated by deploying the model' - widely acknowledged but no turnkey solution.
Amdocs implemented custom NVIDIA AI Blueprint data flywheel - proves enterprise demand but highlights lack of off-the-shelf solution.
OpenPipe's entire business model (capture prod logs -> fine-tune) validates this need, but only covers a fraction of the flywheel.
Shakti SLMs (100M-500M params) demonstrate real demand for edge-optimized domain models in finance, legal, healthcare.
Dell predicts 'The Power of Small: Edge AI Predictions for 2026' - major enterprise demand for edge SLMs.
2026 research: 'memory bandwidth, not compute, is the binding constraint' for on-device LLMs - specialized tooling needed.
r/LocalLLaMA 266K members running models locally demonstrates massive grassroots demand for local/edge deployment.

validated_gaps.title: Domain-Adaptive Continued Pretraining as a Service

demand_signals.signal: March 2026 paper 'The Finetuner's Fallacy: When to Pretrain with Your Finetuning Data' demonstrates that CPT is underused and misunderstood.
Research explores 'training strategies, scaling, model merging and synergistic capabilities' for domain adaptation - CPT is key.
Most platforms only support SFT/LoRA. CPT is 'the most effective way to deeply adapt a model' but requires complex setup.
SaaS companies need to serve multiple client-specific models. Predibase LoRAX addresses serving but not management.
TrueFoundry's MCP server provisioning and AI Gateway hint at demand for multi-tenant model management.

validated_gaps.title: Compliance-Aware Training Pipeline for Regulated Industries

demand_signals.signal: Healthcare, finance, legal companies 'especially preferring fine-tuned LLMs' but face compliance hurdles.
'Regulated industries can't send sensitive data to external APIs' - drives need for on-premise compliance-integrated solutions.
Domain-specific LLMs is fastest-growing segment at 35.1% CAGR, driven by regulated industry needs.
Catastrophic forgetting cited as major challenge: 'fine-tuning on a specific task overwrites parameter configurations supporting other capabilities'.
Paper: 'Fine-Tuning Lowers Safety and Disrupts Evaluation Consistency' - directly validates safety/forgetting concern.
Multiple HN discussions about fine-tuning degrading model quality on general tasks.
'Specialized fields have terminology that vanilla models might not fully grasp—legal texts, medical notes, etc.'
SEED project at MIT CSAIL uses LLMs for domain-specific data curation modules, hinting at ontology-guided approaches.
Discussion on 'when fine-tuning makes sense' shows developers confused about when/how to fine-tune effectively.
'Why You Should Not Fine-Tune Models in 2025' article highlights confusion about when fine-tuning is appropriate.
Most teams waste compute on wrong approach. Fine-tuning covers 80% of use cases at $10-$100 per run, but teams don't know this.

validated_gaps.title: Collaborative Domain Expert Annotation and RLHF Platform

demand_signals.signal: Labelbox and Kili Technology growing in fine-tuning data annotation, but separate from training platforms.
'SLMs can learn effectively from a few hundred well curated examples' - expert curation is critical for small data scenarios.
Fine-tuning success depends on domain experts who 'can be slow and expensive' to engage.
